purpose: Step-by-step recipe or food hero for Ramadan-specific dishes. Extends the beverage/food TF13 family for the most important F&B content window.

tone_descriptor.description: Tone direction: contemplative, bold, warm, dignified, kinetic, etc.

dish_name.description: Name of the Ramadan dish to hero — e.g. harees, jareesh, umm ali, samboosa

prompt_template: Warm Ramadan recipe hero photography of {dish_name} — traditional Saudi or Levantine dish plated for iftar, warm tungsten amber lighting suggesting post-sunset, steam or freshness visual cues, saffron/gold color accent in styling, authentic serving vessel (brass, ceramic, traditional), one or two garnish elements, no people, overhead or 45-degree angle, f/5.6 depth showing full dish context, just-after-Maghreb warmth {cultural_constraints_injection}
anti_patterns: Avoid in output: bright daylight or cold lighting (must feel post-sunset warmth)
Avoid in output: modern minimalist plating that erases Saudi identity
notes: Intents: all. Saudi adaptation guidance: Ramadan is the single most important content window for F&B brands in Saudi Arabia. Recipe hero shots should communicate just-after-iftar warmth (post-Maghreb amber light). Key Ramadan dishes for Saudi context: harees, jareesh, saleeg, kabsa, samboosa, umm ali, kunafa, qatayef, dates in all preparations. Saffron and gold color accents are culturally resonant Ramadan styling signals. Serving vessels: brass trays, ceramic bowls, traditional platters. Recommended frequency: daily during Ramadan for active F&B brands. Compliance level: elevated.
